subject: Mathematics
source: National Curriculum Framework for School Education (NCF-SE), Section 3.4.1.1

CG-1.label: Number Sense, Place Value and Operations
CG-1.description: Understands numbers (counting numbers and fractions), represents whole numbers using the Indian place value system, understands and carries out the four basic operations with whole numbers, and discovers and recognises patterns in number sequences

competency_codes.C-1.1: Represents numbers using the place value structure of the Indian number system, compares whole numbers, and knows and can read the names of very large numbers
competency_codes.C-1.2: Represents and compares commonly used fractions in daily life (such as ½, ¼) as parts of unit wholes, as locations on number lines and as divisions of whole numbers
competency_codes.C-1.3: Understands and visualises arithmetic operations and the relationships among them, knows addition and multiplication tables at least up to 10x10 (pahade) and applies the four basic operations on whole numbers to solve daily life problems
competency_codes.C-1.4: Recognises, describes, and extends simple number patterns such as odd numbers, even numbers, square numbers, cubes, powers of 2, powers of 10, and Virahanka–Fibonacci numbers

CG-2.label: Geometry: Shapes, Spatial Reasoning and Symmetry
CG-2.description: Analyses the characteristics and properties of two- and three-dimensional geometric shapes, specifies locations and describes spatial relationships, and recognises and creates shapes that have symmetry

competency_codes.C-2.1: Identifies, compares, and analyses attributes of two- and three-dimensional shapes and develops vocabulary to describe their attributes/properties
competency_codes.C-2.2: Describes location and movement using both common language and mathematical vocabulary; understands the notion of map (najri naksha)
competency_codes.C-2.3: Recognises and creates symmetry (reflection, rotation) in familiar 2D and 3D shapes
competency_codes.C-2.4: Discovers, recognises, describes, and extends patterns in 2D and 3D shapes

CG-3.label: Measurement: Units, Estimation and Area
CG-3.description: Understands measurable attributes of objects and the units, systems, and processes of such measurement, including those related to distance, length, weight, area, volume, and time using non-standard and standard units

competency_codes.C-3.1: Measures in non-standard and standard units and evaluates the need for standard units
competency_codes.C-3.2: Uses an appropriate unit and tool for the attribute (like length, perimeter, time, weight, volume) being measured
competency_codes.C-3.3: Carries out simple unit conversions, such as from centimetres to metres, within a system of measurement
competency_codes.C-3.4: Understands the definition and formula for the area of a square or rectangle as length times breadth
competency_codes.C-3.5: Devises strategies for estimating the distance, length, time, perimeter (for regular and irregular shapes), area (for regular and irregular shapes), weight, and volume and verifies the same using standard units
competency_codes.C-3.6: Deduces that shapes having equal areas can have different perimeters and shapes having equal perimeters can have different areas
competency_codes.C-3.7: Evaluates the conservation of attributes like length and volume, and solves daily-life problems related to them

CG-4.label: Problem Solving and Computational Thinking
CG-4.description: Develops problem-solving skills with procedural fluency to solve mathematical puzzles as well as daily-life problems, and as a step towards developing computational thinking

competency_codes.C-4.1: Solves puzzles and daily-life problems involving one or more operations on whole numbers (including word puzzles and puzzles from 'recreational' areas, such as the construction of magic squares)
competency_codes.C-4.2: Learns to systematically count and list all possible permutations or combinations given a constraint, in simple situations (e.g., how to make a committee of two people from a group of five people)
competency_codes.C-4.3: Selects appropriate methods and tools for computing with whole numbers, such as mental computation, estimation, or paper-pencil calculation, in accordance with the context

CG-5.label: History of Mathematics: Indian Place Value System
CG-5.description: Knows and appreciates the development in India of the decimal place value system that is used around the world today

competency_codes.C-5.1: Understands the development of zero in India and the Indian place value system for writing numerals, the history of its transmission to the world, and its modern impact on our lives and in all technology
